commit.message:
fix: Use dynamic log file name based on product name

Fixes incorrect log file path display in Settings for CodeLayer PRO and
other build variants. Previously hardcoded to "codelayer.log", now
dynamically uses the product name (e.g., "CodeLayer-Pro.log",
"CodeLayer-Nightly.log").

- Add get_log_file_name() Tauri command to retrieve product-specific filename
- Update SettingsDialog to use dynamic log filename
- Update documentation with correct paths for all variants

Fixes #884
add workshop from gist

commit.message: linter fixes
update package.json

test: Add comprehensive unit tests for profile resolution logic

- Add 34 unit tests covering all profile-related functionality
- Test resolveProfileForRepo() with all edge cases (string, object, invalid profiles)
- Test helper functions (getRepoNameFromMapping, getProfileNameFromMapping)
- Test profile validation and name sanitization
- Test backward compatibility with legacy configs
- All 61 tests passing (27 existing + 34 new)

Coverage includes:
- Profile resolution for string/object mappings
- Fallback behavior for invalid profiles
- Mixed legacy and new format mappings
- Edge cases (undefined profiles, empty configs, special characters)
- Profile name sanitization rules

Refs: #843

docs: Add multi-profile thoughts repository documentation

- Add comprehensive profile management section to README
- Include examples of profile creation, usage, and management
- Document --profile flag for init command
- Explain profile features and use cases
- Provide configuration examples

Refs: #843

feat: Update existing commands to be profile-aware

- init: Add --profile option and early profile resolution (tempProfileConfig)
- init: Use profile config for directory listings and path messages
- init: Write object-format mappings when profile specified
- sync: Resolve profile and use correct thoughtsRepo for git operations
- status: Display profile information and use profile's repo for git status
- config: Show profiles and handle new mapping format in display
- uninit: Handle object-format mappings and show correct profile repo path

All commands now automatically detect and use the correct profile based on
repository mappings. Includes fix for bug where init command was using default
thoughtsRepo for messages when --profile was specified.

Refs: #843

feat: Add profile management commands

- Add 'humanlayer thoughts profile create' command with interactive and non-interactive modes
- Add 'humanlayer thoughts profile list' command with JSON output support
- Add 'humanlayer thoughts profile show' command for viewing profile details
- Add 'humanlayer thoughts profile delete' command with safety checks
- Register all profile subcommands in thoughts command group
- Support profile validation, sanitization, and usage tracking

Users can now create and manage multiple named thoughts profiles for different
organizational contexts (personal, client A, client B, etc.).

Refs: #843

feat: Add type system and core resolution logic for multi-profile thoughts support

- Add RepoMappingObject and ProfileConfig types to support profile-based configuration
- Extend ConfigFile type to include optional profiles field
- Update ThoughtsConfig interface to support both string and object mapping formats
- Add ResolvedProfileConfig interface for profile resolution results
- Implement resolveProfileForRepo() function for automatic profile detection
- Add helper functions for mapping format conversion and profile validation
- Update all path resolution functions with function overloads for backward compatibility

Refs: #843

commit.message:
Capture parent_tool_use_id for all conversation event types

Previously only tool_use events captured parent_tool_use_id from the stream event,
causing sub-agent messages to appear as regular user messages in the UI. Now all
event types (text, tool_result, thinking, system) properly capture this field to
enable distinguishing sub-agent activity from actual user input.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Capture parent_tool_use_id for all conversation event types

Previously only tool_use events captured parent_tool_use_id from the stream event,
causing sub-agent messages to appear as regular user messages in the UI. Now all
event types (text, tool_result, thinking, system) properly capture this field to
enable distinguishing sub-agent activity from actual user input.

commit.message:
Show count in pending approval bar when multiple approvals exist

Updates the pending approval status bar to display the count of pending
approvals when there are multiple (e.g. "3 Pending Approvals" vs "Pending
Approval"). This helps users understand how many approvals are waiting.

commit.message:
Simplify session creation flow by navigating directly to draft page

- Remove session launcher modal usage from Create button
- Navigate directly to /sessions/draft when Create is clicked
- Hide Create button in empty state for normal/drafts views (keep for archived)
- Remove unused openSessionLauncher import

This provides a more direct user experience for creating new sessions.

feat: add WIP limit of 5 for research in review tickets

commit.message:
feat: add WIP limit of 5 for research in review tickets

- Add configurable RESEARCH_WIP_LIMIT environment variable (set to 5)
- Check current tickets in 'research in review' status before fetching new work
- Calculate available capacity and only fetch tickets up to that limit
- Prevent new work when at or above WIP limit
- Add error handling for Linear API failures
- Validate jq output to ensure proper number parsing
- Add GitHub Actions notice and warning annotations for monitoring
- Show warnings when approaching or at WIP limit
- Default to safe state (no capacity) on errors

This creates a simple kanban pull system ensuring that at most 5 tickets
are in the 'research in review' status at any given time, preventing
bottlenecks in the review process.
Reduce undo toast auto-dismiss timeout from 30s to 10s

Fix bash command denials to display in red with denial reason

- Add ApprovalStatus.Denied check to detect denied commands
- Apply terminal error color to entire denial message
- Prefix denied messages with 'Denial Reason:' for clarity
- Hide expand hint for denied commands
- Matches display behavior of other tool components

Fixes ENG-2176

commit.message:
Add Create button to SessionTablePage for quick session creation

The button provides a visible UI element for creating new sessions alongside
the existing keyboard shortcut (c). This improves discoverability and usability
for users who prefer clicking over keyboard shortcuts.

feat(wui): update input placeholders to prompt ENTER key

Update ActiveSessionInput, DraftLauncherInput, and ResponseEditor to show
"ENTER to start typing…" when unfocused. This guides users to press ENTER
to begin typing, improving discoverability of the input interaction pattern.

Also update DraftLauncherInput default placeholder to "Find a bug and fix it."
for better onboarding experience.

Update ActiveSessionInput, DraftLauncherInput, and ResponseEditor to show
"ENTER to start typing…" when unfocused. This guides users to press ENTER
to begin typing, improving discoverability of the input interaction pattern.

Also update DraftLauncherInput default placeholder to "Find a bug and fix it."
for better onboarding experience.

commit.message:
fix(hld): improve session search to match UI display logic

Update SearchSessionsByTitle to:
- Search across title, summary, and query fields (matching UI fallback display)
- Filter to only leaf sessions (no children)
- Exclude archived, draft, and discarded sessions

This ensures cmd+k session search in WUI finds sessions that display their
query/summary text when no explicit title is set.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: disable autocomplete/autocorrect in ResponseEditor

Adds attributes to disable autocomplete, autocorrect, and autocapitalize
in the TipTap-based ResponseEditor to prevent unwanted text suggestions
and corrections, particularly on macOS/Safari.

- Sets spellcheck, autocorrect, autocomplete, and autocapitalize to off/false
- Applies attributes both in TipTap editor configuration and EditorContent component
- Adds documentation explaining Safari's known limitations with contenteditable elements
- Provides workaround suggestions for affected Safari users

Addresses: ENG-2261

feat(linear): Update ResearchBot to output terse comments only

Modify the Claude prompt in the linear-research-tickets workflow to
instruct it to output only a terse format containing:
- Research completion message with document link
- Open questions section

This eliminates verbose conversation history from Linear comments
while preserving essential information.

Implements ENG-2240

commit.message:
Updates tests to use Claude Code's actual default slash commands location

The previous commit fixed GetSlashCommands to respect CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR and use
the correct default location (~/.config/claude-code/commands). These test updates
ensure tests work with that fix by:

- Unsetting CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR for proper test isolation
- Using ~/.config/claude-code/commands instead of the old ~/.claude/commands path
- Properly restoring environment variables in defer blocks

This ensures tests accurately validate the new behavior and don't pick up
real configuration during test runs.

- Unsetting CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR for proper test isolation
- Using ~/.config/claude-code/commands instead of the old ~/.claude/commands path

commit.message:
feat(wui): add directory validation and creation for additional directories

Add real-time directory validation and automatic creation capabilities to the
Additional Directories dropdown, providing a smoother UX when adding new paths.

Backend changes:
- Add POST /directories endpoint to create directories via daemon
- Add CreateDirectory handler with tilde expansion and os.MkdirAll
- Regenerate Go and TypeScript SDKs from OpenAPI spec
- Add validateDirectory and createDirectory methods to SDK client

Frontend changes:
- Add debounced validation (300ms) as user types directory paths
- Track directory existence state and show visual feedback
- Dynamic button text: "Add" for existing dirs, "Create" for non-existent
- Use destructive button variant (red) when creating directories
- Improved toast messages distinguishing create vs add operations
- Fix FuzzySearchInput bug: now correctly filters out files, showing only directories

All filesystem operations go through daemon endpoints (no Tauri fs calls),
ensuring consistent behavior and proper permission handling.

All filesystem operations go through daemon endpoints (no Tauri fs calls),

commit.message:
feat(wui): add directory creation dialog for draft launch flow

Implement client-side handling for directory creation when launching draft sessions:

- Add CreateDirectoryDialog component with confirmation UI
- Update DraftLauncherForm to catch 422 responses and show dialog
- Update HTTP client to parse 422 directory_not_found errors
- Regenerate SDK with createDirectoryIfNotExists parameter support
- Add CREATE_DIRECTORY_DIALOG hotkey scope

When launching a draft session with a non-existent working directory, the daemon returns 422 with directory details. The UI now shows a confirmation dialog and retries the launch with createDirectoryIfNotExists=true when confirmed.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(hld): defer directory validation for draft sessions to launch time

Allows draft sessions to be configured with any working directory path without immediate validation. Directory validation and creation now happens when launching the draft via POST /sessions/{id}/launch, not during draft creation. This improves UX by keeping sessions in draft state on validation failure.

- Skip directory validation in LaunchSession when isDraft=true
- Add createDirectoryIfNotExists parameter to LaunchDraftSession API
- Validate/create directories before status transition in LaunchDraftSession
- Return 422 response when directory doesn't exist and creation not requested
- Update OpenAPI spec and regenerate server code

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(hld): add support for creating working directories on session launch

Add directory validation and automatic creation support for Claude Code sessions:
- New /validate-directory endpoint to check directory status before session creation
- Add createDirectoryIfNotExists flag to session creation API
- Implement directory creation logic in session manager with proper error handling
- Return 422 status when directory doesn't exist and needs creation
- Add DirectoryNotFoundError type for cleaner error handling

This allows Claude Code to prompt users before creating new directories,
improving the user experience when launching sessions in non-existent paths.

- New /validate-directory endpoint to check directory status before session creation
- Add createDirectoryIfNotExists flag to session creation API
- Implement directory creation logic in session manager with proper error handling
- Return 422 status when directory doesn't exist and needs creation
- Add DirectoryNotFoundError type for cleaner error handling

This allows Claude Code to prompt users before creating new directories,
improving the user experience when launching sessions in non-existent paths.

commit.message:
Fixes GetSlashCommands to respect CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR environment variable

Previously, GetSlashCommands was hardcoded to look for global slash commands
in ~/.claude/commands, ignoring the CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR environment variable.

This fix ensures that:
- When CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR is set, global commands are loaded from
  $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/commands
- When not set, falls back to the default Claude Code location at
  ~/.config/claude-code/commands

Implemented using TDD: tests were written first (failing), then the fix
was applied, and tests now pass.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add codelayer CLI aliases with app launch detection (Phase 3)

Implement invocation-aware behavior for codelayer and codelayer-nightly CLI commands:
- Add invocation detection utilities to determine binary name at runtime
- Launch CodeLayer app when invoked as codelayer/codelayer-nightly with no arguments
- Dynamically select daemon socket based on invocation name (nightly vs stable)
- Add socket path logging to launch command for debugging
- Register codelayer and codelayer-nightly bin entries in package.json

When users run just `codelayer` with no arguments, the app opens automatically.
Running with arguments (e.g., `codelayer launch "help"`) works as normal CLI.
The nightly variant uses ~/.humanlayer/daemon-nightly.sock automatically.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement invocation-aware behavior for codelayer and codelayer-nightly CLI commands:
- Add invocation detection utilities to determine binary name at runtime
- Launch CodeLayer app when invoked as codelayer/codelayer-nightly with no arguments
- Dynamically select daemon socket based on invocation name (nightly vs stable)
- Add socket path logging to launch command for debugging
- Register codelayer and codelayer-nightly bin entries in package.json

When users run just `codelayer` with no arguments, the app opens automatically.
Running with arguments (e.g., `codelayer launch "help"`) works as normal CLI.
The nightly variant uses ~/.humanlayer/daemon-nightly.sock automatically.

commit.message:
Add codelayer binary aliases to workflow cask generation

Update the GitHub workflow to include codelayer and codelayer-nightly
binary symlinks when generating Homebrew cask files. This ensures the
aliases are included in automated releases.

- Stable: Added 'codelayer' symlink alongside 'humanlayer'
- Nightly: Added 'codelayer-nightly' symlink alongside 'humanlayer-nightly'

Part of ENG-2327

fix(ui): improve undo toast behavior and state management

- Add React import and fix toast positioning to top-right
- Change from success toasts to default variant for undo operations
- Add flash effect to undo button on click (100ms)
- Fix toast dismissal after undo operation
- Remove unwanted success toasts after undo actions
- Fix draft/session title display in toast descriptions
- Add support for bulk draft restoration with new API endpoint
- Update z hotkey handler to support bulk operation toast IDs
- Improve event handler to prevent stale closures in undo callbacks

feat(sdk): add bulk draft restoration API support

Add generated SDK models and API methods for the bulkRestoreDrafts endpoint that allows restoring multiple discarded draft sessions back to draft status. This enables undo functionality for bulk draft deletion operations.

Enhance error handling to display backend error messages in WUI

- Make formatError async to handle ResponseError from SDK
- Parse JSON error responses and extract structured error messages
- Update all formatError calls to use async/await
- Properly display backend error messages with code and description
- Remove extra whitespace in Layout.tsx

This ensures users see clear, actionable error messages from the backend
rather than generic error text.

Fix MCP tool calls with empty args showing empty JSON blocks

Don't render MCPToolCallParamPreview component when toolInput is an empty object, preventing unnecessary empty JSON blocks in the UI. Also cleaned up minor formatting in Layout.tsx.

Remove empty line in Layout.tsx hotkey configuration

Minor formatting cleanup in the command+r reload hotkey configuration.

Add copy button to working directory in session header

Added a copy-to-clipboard button next to the working directory display
in the session header. The button appears on hover and allows users
to easily copy the current working directory path.

- Imported Copy icon and copyToClipboard utility
- Added hover-visible copy button with proper styling
- Button appears inline with the directory path
- Updated text styling for better visibility

commit.message:
fix(ui): cmd+r hotkey moved to implicit global scope and enabled on content editable

this fixes an issue where the cmd+r hotkey was in the root scope, which means that it would not be available while modals or dialogs are open. additionally, it is enabled when a contentEditable element is focused, such that it can be triggered while the user has the prompt input or another input open

commit.message:
Move window refresh shortcuts from Tauri to React layer

Refactor refresh functionality to use React's useHotkeys hook instead of
Tauri's global shortcut system. This provides better control and keeps
the hotkey logic with the UI code.

- Remove Rust-based CommandOrControl+r shortcut registration
- Add cmd+r for normal reload and cmd+shift+r for force reload
- Apply shortcuts to ROOT scope with preventDefault enabled

the hotkey logic with the UI code.

commit.message: fix stuff

feat(telemetry): add defensive error handling to usePostHogTracking hook

Wrap trackEvent and trackPageView functions with try-catch blocks to prevent
tracking failures from breaking the application. Errors are logged to console
with context but never thrown to calling components.

- Add try-catch around all operations in trackEvent
- Add try-catch around all operations in trackPageView
- Include detailed error logging with event names for debugging
- Ensure tracking failures never propagate to UI components

releases.name: codelayer-0.1.0-nightly-20260227142452
releases.body:
# CodeLayer Nightly Release

Version: 0.1.0-nightly-20260227142452

## ⚠️ Nightly Build Warning\n\nThis is a nightly build with the latest features and fixes. It may contain unstable features.\n



## Installation

### Homebrew (Recommended)
```bash
brew tap humanlayer/humanlayer
brew install --cask codelayer-nightly
```

### Manual Installation
1. Download the DMG file below
2. Open the DMG and drag CodeLayer-Nightly to Applications
3. Launch CodeLayer-Nightly from Applications

## Troubleshooting

If you encounter "damaged app" warnings on macOS:
```bash
xattr -rc /Applications/CodeLayer-Nightly.app
```

## Logs

Logs can be found at:
```
~/Library/Logs/dev.humanlayer.wui.nightly/CodeLayer-Nightly.log
```

readme_html:
The best way to get Coding Agents to solve hard problems in complex codebases

CodeLayer is an open source IDE that lets you orchestrate AI coding agents.

It comes with battle-tested workflows that enable AI to solve hard problems in large, complex codebases.

Built on Claude Code. Open source. Scale from your laptop to your entire team.

Join Waitlist | Discord

"Our entire company is using CodeLayer now. We're shipping one banger PR after the other. It is so f-ing good. Unbelievable dude."

– René Brandel, Founder @ Casco (YC X25)

  • Superhuman for Claude Code - Keyboard-first workflows designed for builders who value speed and control.

  • Advanced Context Engineering - Scale AI-first dev to your entire team, without devolving into a chaotic slop-fest.

  • M U L T I C L A U D E - Run Claude Code sessions in parallel. Worktrees? Done. Remote cloud workers? You got it.

"This has improved my productivity (and token consumption) by at least 50%. Taking a superhuman style approach just makes soo much sense. Also, its so freaking cool to look back at all the work you've done in a day."

– Tyler Brown, Founder @ Revlo.ai

From the team that brought you "Context Engineering"

Leading experts on getting the most out of today's models.

Advanced Context Engineering for Coding Agents

This talk, given at YC on August 20th, 2025 lays out the groundwork for using AI to solve hard problems in complex codebases.

  • GitHub
  • YouTube

12 Factor Agents

A set of principles for building reliable and scalable LLM applications, inspired by the original 12-Factor App methodology.

  • GitHub
  • YouTube

The original repo that coined the term "context engineering" back in April 2025.

🦄 AI That Works

A weekly conversation about how we can all get the most juice out of todays models with @hellovai & @dexhorthy

  • GitHub
  • Podcast

For Teams

Invest in outcomes, not tools.

Want to scale AI-first development to your entire org? Get tailored workflows, custom integrations, and cutting-edge advice.

HumanLayer's expert engineers will ship in the trenches with you and your team until everyone is a 100x engineer.

📧 Shoot us an email at contact@humanlayer.dev, mention your team size and current AI development stack.

Quick Start

# Coming soon - join the waitlist for early access
npx humanlayer join-waitlist --email ...

Legacy Documentation

Looking for the HumanLayer SDK documentation? See humanlayer.md

Contributing

CodeLayer and the HumanLayer SDK are open-source and we welcome contributions in the form of issues, documentation, pull requests, and more. See CONTRIBUTING.md for more details.

License

The HumanLayer SDK and CodeLayer sources in this repo are licensed under the Apache 2 License.
